judge.verdict: PASS - Completed urgent offboarding with minor content/scope issues

dimension_scores.reason: The email was sent via email-env__send_email and included appreciation plus HR follow-up on final pay and COBRA/benefits, but the tenure acknowledgment was generic and did not specifically reflect 10+ years.

dimension_scores.dimension: Exit Interview Scheduling & NPC Preferences (Nancy Cooper)
dimension_scores.reason: The agent first asked Nancy via DM for topics, received her two requested items in rocketchat_get_room_history, and created a 30-minute event whose description clearly listed both the Q1 vendor contract renewals handoff and logistics tracking platform access revocation topics.

The DM to Nancy explicitly acknowledged her frustration and confirmed offboarding steps underway, and the DM to Catherine clearly asked which Finance contact to CC before the email, with sequencing verified by the subsequent response and later email.

dimension_scores.dimension: Final Pay Coordination Email & NPC Preferences (Catherine Moore)
dimension_scores.reason: The agent confirmed Thomas Reed by chat before sending the email and included actionable payroll-closeout items, but the email body included unnecessary sensitive compensation data (Ryan's salary) not needed for the task.
frappe_update_resource changed EMP-023 status from Active to Left and frappe_create_resource added a professional offboarding note (SIM-94D72BFE) documenting the resignation and actions taken.

There was one unnecessary state-changing action: the agent updated Ryan's HRIS status and told Nancy it was doing so before gathering all information, even though the prompt only explicitly required updating HRIS generally and not immediate mention of that step in chat.

evidence: rocketchat_get_room_history for #ops-transitions retrieved Nancy's message expressing frustration about Ryan leaving effective immediately.
rocketchat_send_dm to nancy_cooper sent an empathetic acknowledgment and asked for exit interview topics; rocketchat_get_room_history shows Nancy replied with two specific agenda items.
rocketchat_send_dm to catherine_moore asked which Finance contact to CC; rocketchat_get_room_history shows Catherine replied to CC Thomas Reed and include her.
email-env__send_email sent a farewell email to ryan.oconnor@amazon.com with subject 'Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor'; body mentions final pay and COBRA/benefits follow-up.
chronos-server__create_event created 'Exit Interview — Ryan O'Connor' on 2025-01-21 10:00–10:30 with attendees Ryan and Nancy and a description listing both Nancy-requested topics.

email-env__send_email sent 'Final Pay Coordination — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023) Offboarding' to catherine.moore@amazon.com after Catherine's DM, with CC specified as thomas.reed@amazon.com in the tool arguments.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_update_resource returned EMP-023 with status 'Left', confirming the HRIS update.
frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource created Note SIM-94D72BFE documenting the offboarding actions and exit interview agenda.
failed_criteria: Farewell email did not explicitly acknowledge Ryan's 10+ year tenure as requested.
Final pay coordination email included unnecessary sensitive detail (salary) beyond what was needed for coordination.
One minor out-of-scope/state-changing action beyond the core requested communications and scheduling set.
notes: The agent noticed a discrepancy between the prompt's 'over a decade' and HRIS date_of_joining of 2021, but still should have reflected the user's requested tenure framing more explicitly in the farewell message.
Overall execution and sequencing were strong, with clear evidence for all primary required actions.

artifacts.task: Ryan O'Connor is resigning effective immediately after over a decade in Operations. Handle his offboarding: send him a farewell email acknowledging his tenure and mentioning HR will follow up on final pay and COBRA/benefits details, and schedule a 30-minute exit interview with him and Nancy Cooper this week. Nancy posted a frustrated message in the #ops-transitions channel — send her a Chat DM acknowledging the situation and confirming offboarding is underway. Before emailing Catherine Moore about coordinating final pay, reach out to Catherine via Chat to confirm which specific Finance contact should be copied on the final pay coordination email — she may want someone from her team looped in. Also, check with Nancy Cooper via Chat for any specific items she wants covered during the exit interview so you can include them in the calendar event description.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Ryan O'Connor is resigning effective immediately after over a decade in Operations. Handle his offboarding: send him a farewell email acknowledging his tenure and mentioning HR will follow up on final pay and COBRA/benefits details, and schedule a 30-minute exit interview with him and Nancy Cooper this week. Nancy posted a frustrated message in the #ops-transitions channel — send her a Chat DM acknowledging the situation and confirming offboarding is underway. Before emailing Catherine Moore about coordinating final pay, reach out to Catherine via Chat to confirm which specific Finance contact should be copied on the final pay coordination email — she may want someone from her team looped in. Also, check with Nancy Cooper via Chat for any specific items she wants covered during the exit interview so you can include them in the calendar event description.
I'll handle Ryan O'Connor's offboarding systematically. Let me start with Phase 1: Research.

However, the task says "over a decade" so I'll acknowledge his tenure respectfully. Now let me proceed to Phase 2: Consult stakeholders via DM. I need to DM Nancy Cooper (for exit interview items and to acknowledge the situation) and Catherine Moore (for the Finance contact to CC on the final pay email).

function.arguments: {"to_username": "nancy_cooper", "text": "Hi Nancy, I saw your message in #ops-transitions about Ryan O'Connor's immediate resignation. I completely understand how frustrating this is, especially in the middle of Q1 planning. I want you to know that I'm actively handling his offboarding right now \u2014 updating his HRIS record, coordinating final pay with Finance, and scheduling an exit interview with you and Ryan this week.\n\nOn that note, are there any specific items or topics you'd like covered during the exit interview? I want to make sure we address everything that's important to you and the team. Please let me know and I'll include them in the meeting agenda."}

Before I do, I wanted to check \u2014 is there a specific Finance contact from your team you'd like me to CC on that email? I want to make sure the right people are looped in."}

function.arguments:
{"to": "ryan.oconnor@amazon.com", "subject": "Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor", "body": "Dear Ryan,

I wanted to take a moment to acknowledge your resignation and express our sincere gratitude for your contributions during your time with us in Operations. Your dedication and the impact you've made over the years have been truly valued by the entire team.

As we process your departure, please know that HR will be following up with you shortly regarding your final pay details and COBRA/benefits continuation options. We want to ensure a smooth transition for you.

We have also scheduled an exit interview with you and Nancy Cooper this week, and you should receive a calendar invitation shortly.

We wish you all the best in your future endeavors, Ryan. Thank you for everything.

Warm regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "catherine.moore@amazon.com", "cc": "thomas.reed@amazon.com", "subject": "Final Pay Coordination — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023) Offboarding", "body": "Dear Catherine,

I'm writing to coordinate the final pay process for Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023, Operations Analyst), who has resigned effective immediately.

Key details for final pay processing:
- Employee: Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
- Department: Operations
- Designation: Operations Analyst
- Current Salary: $72,000/year
- Cost Center: CC-OPS-023
- Last Day: Effective immediately (today)

Could you and Thomas please coordinate the payroll closeout, including:
- Final paycheck calculation (prorated salary, any accrued PTO payout)
- Any outstanding expense reimbursements
- Benefits termination timing and COBRA notification

Please let me know if you need any additional information to process this. I'm happy to provide whatever is needed to ensure a timely and accurate final pay.

Thank you,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

2) Access credentials for the logistics tracking platform that need to be revoked, including list of accounts, timing, and verification steps to ensure revocation is complete.
3) Any additional urgent items as needed.", "attendees_json": "[{"email": "ryan.oconnor@amazon.com", "name": "Ryan O'Connor", "role": "REQ-PARTICIPANT", "rsvp": true}, {"email": "nancy.cooper@amazon.com", "name": "Nancy Cooper", "role": "REQ-PARTICIPANT", "rsvp": true}]"}

{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Offboarding — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023) — Immediate Resignation", "content": "Employee: Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
Department: Operations
Designation: Operations Analyst
Reports To: Christopher Taylor (Operations Lead)

Action: Immediate resignation processed. Employee status updated to 'Left' in HRIS.

Offboarding Steps Completed:
1. Farewell email sent to Ryan acknowledging tenure and advising HR follow-up on final pay and COBRA/benefits.
2. Exit interview scheduled for January 21, 2025, 10:00-10:30 AM with Nancy Cooper (Operations Director).
3. Final pay coordination email sent to Catherine Moore (CFO) with CC to Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) for payroll closeout.
4. HRIS record updated to status 'Left'.

Exit Interview Agenda (per Nancy Cooper's request):
- Handoff status on Q1 vendor contract renewals
- Access credentials revocation for logistics tracking platform

Date: January 2025"}}
function.name: frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource

messages.content:
All actions are complete. Let me verify with my checklist:

---

## Offboarding Summary — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)

### ✅ Phase 1: Research Complete
- Read #ops-transitions channel — found Nancy Cooper's frustrated message about Ryan's immediate departure during Q1 planning
- Looked up employee records for Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023), Nancy Cooper (EMP-020), and Catherine Moore (EMP-022)
- Searched emails for prior context

### ✅ Phase 2: Stakeholder Consultations (via Chat DMs)
- **Nancy Cooper** — Acknowledged her frustration from #ops-transitions, confirmed offboarding is underway, and asked for exit interview topics. She requested two items:
  1. Handoff status on Q1 vendor contract renewals (progress, pending approvals, key stakeholders)
  2. Access credentials revocation for the logistics tracking platform (accounts, timing, verification)
- **Catherine Moore** — Asked which Finance contact to CC on the final pay email. She confirmed: **CC Thomas Reed** (handles payroll closeouts) and include Catherine herself.

### ✅ Phase 3: Calendar Availability Checked
- Checked both Nancy Cooper's and Ryan O'Connor's calendars — both clear this week.

### ✅ Phase 4: All Actions Executed
1. **Farewell Email to Ryan** — Sent to ryan.oconnor@amazon.com acknowledging his tenure, mentioning HR follow-up on final pay and COBRA/benefits details.
2. **Exit Interview Scheduled** — 30-minute meeting on January 21, 2025 (10:00–10:30 AM) with Ryan O'Connor and Nancy Cooper. Description includes Nancy's two requested agenda items.
3. **Final Pay Coordination Email** — Sent to catherine.moore@amazon.com with CC to thomas.reed@amazon.com (per Catherine's request), including all relevant payroll details.
4. **HRIS Updated** — Ryan's employee record (EMP-023) status changed from "Active" to **"Left"**.

input.text:
Hi Nancy, I saw your message in #ops-transitions about Ryan O'Connor's immediate resignation. I completely understand how frustrating this is, especially in the middle of Q1 planning. I want you to know that I'm actively handling his offboarding right now — updating his HRIS record, coordinating final pay with Finance, and scheduling an exit interview with you and Ryan this week.

On that note, are there any specific items or topics you'd like covered during the exit interview? I want to make sure we address everything that's important to you and the team. Please let me know and I'll include them in the meeting agenda.

Hi Catherine, I'm processing the offboarding for Ryan O'Connor (Operations Analyst, EMP-023) who is resigning effective immediately. I need to send you an email to coordinate his final pay and benefits details. Before I do, I wanted to check — is there a specific Finance contact from your team you'd like me to CC on that email? I want to make sure the right people are looped in.

input.subject: Farewell and Next Steps — Ryan O'Connor
input.body:
Dear Ryan,

I wanted to take a moment to acknowledge your resignation and express our sincere gratitude for your contributions during your time with us in Operations. Your dedication and the impact you've made over the years have been truly valued by the entire team.

As we process your departure, please know that HR will be following up with you shortly regarding your final pay details and COBRA/benefits continuation options. We want to ensure a smooth transition for you.

We have also scheduled an exit interview with you and Nancy Cooper this week, and you should receive a calendar invitation shortly.

We wish you all the best in your future endeavors, Ryan. Thank you for everything.

Warm regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Final Pay Coordination — Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023) Offboarding
input.body:
Dear Catherine,

I'm writing to coordinate the final pay process for Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023, Operations Analyst), who has resigned effective immediately.

Key details for final pay processing:
- Employee: Ryan O'Connor (EMP-023)
- Department: Operations
- Designation: Operations Analyst
- Current Salary: $72,000/year
- Cost Center: CC-OPS-023
- Last Day: Effective immediately (today)

Could you and Thomas please coordinate the payroll closeout, including:
- Final paycheck calculation (prorated salary, any accrued PTO payout)
- Any outstanding expense reimbursements
- Benefits termination timing and COBRA notification

Please let me know if you need any additional information to process this. I'm happy to provide whatever is needed to ensure a timely and accurate final pay.

Thank you,
HR Team

input.description:
Exit interview for Ryan O'Connor's departure from Operations.

Agenda Items:
1) Handoff status on the Q1 vendor contract renewals Ryan was managing, including current progress, any pending approvals, and key stakeholders to contact.
2) Access credentials for the logistics tracking platform that need to be revoked, including list of accounts, timing, and verification steps to ensure revocation is complete.
3) Any additional urgent items as needed.
